references.title: A Statistical Analysis of Factors Affecting Higher Education Dropouts

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Dropout at the End of the First-Year - define as not registering in the University of Bari in the Second YEar
extracted.Courses: All courses in University of Bari in 2015/2016
extracted.Data sources:
Highschool Academic Performance,
Number of Credits Completed in First Year
extracted.Features: [Type of Highschool Diploma, Area, Degree, Credits, Age, Diploma Grade]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Authors aggregate all predictors into a single feature vector.
extracted.Comments:
Authors use cost-complexity pruningto optimize their DT. Best alpha found to be at 0.3195.

Credits is the most important predictor. In fact the authors make an exploratory analysis to identify the most important predictors.
extracted.Limitations:
Not approppriate for predictive purposes as requires learners to complete first-year before the model is able to make a prediction.

Limited number of ML algorithms.

annotations.text: The first-to-second year in an undergraduate program is among the most important stages of a student’s academic career (especially when it comes to deciding whether to drop out or not). Authors analyze the factors behind dropout in Italy at two-levels of resolution:
Authors use a set of classification methods to identify the profiles of students who are more likely to dropout.
